steps.title: Find the radius from the circumference
Calculate the cylinder volume
steps.content:
With $r = 3$ and $h = 19$:
$$V = \pi(3)^2(19) = 171\pi \text{ cubic meters}$$
The radius is squared because each circular base has area $\pi r^2$, and the height scales that area through the full cylinder. That confirms C is correct.
